title: hieracium murorum subsp. sudermannicum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. sudermannicum, also known as Sudermann's Hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium murorum plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which provide various health benefits, including antioxidant and immune-boosting properties, as well as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Hieracium murorum subsp. sudermannicum can be prepared as a tincture, decoction, or salve, and is often used topically to soothe and protect the skin.
